title: Phase 3.0 redesign DeltaSetup public project experience
description: Redesign the DeltaSetup public GitHub Pages experience so it is mindblowingly helpful, clear, and visually coherent. Current issues include awkward stat/ticker sections, unclear section purpose, weak rendered contrast in some components, too much decorative narrative without enough decision/action clarity, and inconsistent visual rhythm. Start with experience-architect strategy, then implement focused improvements.
acceptance_criteria: Experience architect review exists; redesign plan is documented; high-impact page sections are refactored for clarity and visual quality; QA/static/browser smoke audits pass; changes pushed to gh-pages.
notes:
Owner/agent: code-puppy-13b0ad. Tyler explicitly requested Phase 3.0 and experience-architect involvement.
web-puppy-0a95fb completed Phase 3.0 B2B/static landing-page research and saved deliverables under research/b2b-static-landing-pages-phase3-redesign/. Findings cover IA, vanity-stat replacement, action-oriented sections, WCAG 2.2 card/button/focus/contrast considerations, and GitHub Pages/no-build constraints.
close_reason: Completed Phase 3.0 pass 1. Invoked experience-architect, documented plan, replaced ugly stats/ticker with Current Decision Brief, simplified hero copy/routing, added Decision Queue, fixed reveal visibility for new decision surfaces, and verified static/browser audits pass. Follow-up passes filed for White Glove/problem evidence compression and MSP homepage routing.

title: Fix rendered MSP page contrast and sidebar view button design
description: The deployed MSP page still has visibly poor rendered contrast and awkward view-switcher buttons despite internal readiness checks. Fix actual visual contrast for Project/Ops/MSP Brief sidebar buttons, MSP hero action buttons, and muted text/card treatments. Validate with browser smoke/static audits and push.
acceptance_criteria: MSP page view switcher is compact and readable; hero action buttons have strong contrast; visible muted copy/cards are readable in rendered screenshots; static and browser smoke audits pass; changes pushed to gh-pages.
notes: Owner/agent: code-puppy-13b0ad. Reported by Tyler with screenshot from https://delta-crown-org.github.io/DeltaSetup/msp.html.
close_reason: Fixed rendered MSP page contrast and navigation button design. Reworked sidebar view switcher into readable stacked pills, increased sidebar text contrast, made MSP hero content immediately visible/readable, strengthened MSP card/body text contrast, bumped cache-busters, and verified static + browser smoke audits pass.

title: Establish ongoing HTT->DCE user-onboarding process (cross-tenant sync or manual)
description:
Yara Gonzalez Ramirez (yara.gonzalez@httbrands.com) was created in HTT on 2024-08-15 with department='HTT Brands Corporate' and licensing, yet she does NOT appear in the DCE tenant via cross-tenant sync — unlike her 33 peers with the same department who all synced correctly.

This indicates either:
(a) Her HTT user object has some flag that excludes her from sync (e.g., hidden from address lists, blocked sign-in at sync time, scoped-out via filter)
(b) The cross-tenant sync configuration has a per-user exception
(c) Her sync was attempted and is in failure state (check sync provisioning logs in Entra)

Action: review the HTT-side cross-tenant sync job (Entra admin center > Identity > External Identities > Cross-tenant sync). Inspect provisioning logs for yara.gonzalez. Resolve the root cause + document for any other potentially-affected users.

Discovered while expanding Crown Connection membership (DeltaSetup-of8).
notes:
2026-05-15 root cause found: HTT and DCE are NOT in a Multi-Tenant Org and have NO cross-tenant synchronization. The 74 HTT-origin users in DCE all trace back to a single B2B-invite bulk event on 2026-03-04 (creationType=Invitation, externalUserState=Accepted, externalUserStateChangeDateTime within 1 second of createdDateTime — all hallmarks of a programmatic silent bulk).

Yara wasn't an anomaly — she was simply not included in that 2026-03-04 bulk. Same as the other 6 missing users. All 7 are now invited and present in DCE.

This bd is now REPURPOSED to the broader ongoing-process need: 'How do new HTT corporate hires get into DCE?' Options:

(a) Set up actual cross-tenant synchronization HTT -> DCE (preferred long-term; configure via Entra Admin Center > External Identities > Cross-tenant sync; requires Entra ID P1 in HTT)

(b) Manual B2B invite as a step in the HTT onboarding checklist (use tools/invite-htt-users-to-dce.py); requires DCE admin or scoped-down delegated permission

(c) Periodic backfill script run (cron): re-runs invite + expansion every N weeks to catch any drift

Recommend (a) for true scalability. (b) and (c) are workable interim until then.

Discovered while resolving DeltaSetup-cwn.
2026-05-15 INVESTIGATION COMPLETE — see docs/architecture/htt-dce-cross-tenant-sync-deep-dive.md

Earlier conclusion ('no cross-tenant sync exists') was WRONG. Cross-tenant sync DOES exist and is active. Updated understanding:

WHAT EXISTS:
- HTT-to-DCE-User-Sync app (appId 9c8934a1-658d-4bab-b7a1-a1a11593a203)
- Microsoft Azure2Azure cross-tenant sync template
- Schedule: PT40M (every 40 min), Active, last steady state 2026-05-15T23:54:02Z
- Same pattern exists for FMNC, TLL; HTT also has MTO with Bishops

THE GATE:
- appRoleAssignmentRequired = true on the sync SP
- One security group assigned: SG-DCE-Sync-Users (group id 6f5cc75e-b2ae-4ed2-992d-e56d4e3ef5f3)
- Currently STATIC, 248 members
- Membership in this group = gets synced to DCE

WHY THE 7 FAILED:
- 6/7 are not in SG-DCE-Sync-Users at all (HR process drift since 2026-03-04 seeding)
- 1/7 (Jill Holderfield) IS in the group but didn't sync — anomaly; check provisioning logs

RECOMMENDED FIX (4.1 in the deep-dive doc):
Make SG-DCE-Sync-Users DYNAMIC with a rule like:
  (user.userPrincipalName -match ".*@httbrands\.com$") -and (user.accountEnabled -eq true)

Pros: zero ongoing maintenance; new hires auto-flow within 40 min; removes the 'did HR remember' failure mode.
Cons: loses ability to exclude specific HTT users; needs P1 in HTT (likely already).
Decision needed: any HTT users that should NOT sync to DCE?

This bd remains open for: (a) Tyler's decision on dynamic-group adoption, (b) optionally diagnose Jill Holderfield, (c) document the HR-onboarding step.
2026-05-15 23:59 UTC RESOLVED IN FULL.

Actions taken:
1. Converted SG-DCE-Sync-Users from STATIC to DYNAMIC via Graph PATCH (HTTP 204). New rule:
   (user.userPrincipalName -match ".*@httbrands\.com$") and (user.accountEnabled -eq true)
   With membershipRuleProcessingState=On.

2. Triggered provisionOnDemand for Jill Holderfield (HTTP 200). She was already in DCE via the earlier B2B invite tonight, so no duplicate created. Confirmed Jill in DCE: jill.holderfield_httbrands.com#EXT#@deltacrown.onmicrosoft.com, userType=Member.

3. Documented full architecture + plan in docs/architecture/dce-sharepoint-design-roadmap.md (which references the deep-dive doc).

Net: HTT->DCE onboarding is now fully automated. Every new enabled HTT user will be picked up by the dynamic rule within minutes, included in the cross-tenant sync within 40 minutes, and become eligible for Crown Connection via tools/expand-crown-connection-htt-corp.py.

Closing this bd.

title: Provision DCE owners-connect SharePoint site (Crown Connection)
description:
Per Tyler 2026-05-15 / Kristin SharePoint-launch ask: build a Delta Crown Extensions equivalent of the existing per-brand owner SharePoint sites (Bishops Connect, Frenchies Studio Connection, TLL Owners Group) so the existing 5 DCE franchise owners have a private space + dedicated mail address.

Decisions captured (Tyler 2026-05-15):
- Name: Crown Connection (display) / CrownConnection (mailNickname) — parallel to Frenchies Studio Connection with Crown brand front
- Primary SMTP: CrownConnection@deltacrown.com
- Site URL: https://deltacrown.sharepoint.com/sites/CrownConnection
- Membership at launch: owners only (TLL pattern) — Sarah Miller, Jay Miller, Allynn Shepherd, Amit Shah, Toni Careccia
- Visibility: Private, not Team-enabled initially
- Group owner: Tyler Granlund - Admin (DCE guest, Global Admin)

Implementation:
- M365 Group provisioned via Graph (DCE Global Admin via az session token)
- SharePoint site auto-provisioned by M365 Group backing (4-sec lag)
- 5 owner members added via owners@odata.bind on POST /v1.0/groups
- All verified via Graph readback

Evidence: .local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T215601Z-crown-connection-FINAL.json

Tooling shipped:
- tools/provision-crown-connection.sh (idempotent provisioning playbook)
- docs/naming-conventions/owners-connect-cross-brand.md (canonical spec for future brand parity)
- docs/onboarding/crown-connection-launch-handoff-jenna-boden.md (handoff memo for Jenna)

Known follow-ups (filed separately): OwnerConnection@ alias, cross-brand cleanup.
notes:
2026-05-15 EXPANSION: per Tyler, expanded Crown Connection access to all licensed @httbrands.com users as MEMBERS (not Visitors). Added Jenna Bowden + Kristin Kidd as group OWNERS.

Cross-tenant sync HTT->DCE is already configured (74 HTT users present in DCE as userType=Member via Multi-Tenant Org pattern). Mapped HTT licensed UPNs to their DCE Member objects and bulk-added.

Final state (.local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T221658Z-crown-connection-htt-corp-EXPANDED.json):
- 50 total members (5 DCE-native franchise owners + 45 HTT corp users)
- 3 owners: Tyler Granlund - Admin, Kristin Kidd, Jenna Bowden

Tooling added: tools/expand-crown-connection-htt-corp.py (idempotent re-runnable; dry-run flag; pulls fresh data on each run, no stale state).

Caveats:
- Jenna BOWDEN, not Boden (Tyler's verbal phonetic — corrected throughout)
- 7 HTT licensed users not yet synced to DCE; see DeltaSetup-<next bead>
- Departments null on 6 of 7 unmapped users (recently created Feb-Apr 2026) — cross-tenant sync should catch on next cycle (~40 min)
- Yara Gonzalez Ramirez is anomalous: has dept='HTT Brands Corporate' + account from 2024-08, should already be synced; needs sync-scope investigation

title: Deliver Friday SharePoint hub basics for Tyler + Jamie
description:
Umbrella issue for the 2026-05-15 Friday SharePoint hub delivery to Tyler Granlund and Jamie Baer per docs/architecture/prompt-deltasetup-sharepoint-hub-friday-2026-05-15.md.

Scope (basic only — Jamie populates branding/content in Dallas working session week of 5/18 with Erica Upshur and Meg Roberts):
- SharePoint site provisioned in HTT tenant under Delta Crown brand domain path
- Hub-and-spoke architecture matching tenant standard (golden-child pattern)
- 11-section folder skeleton (Phase 5 of prompt)
- Navigation structure wired
- Microsoft Teams channels aligned and wired to hub
- Email domain inheritance + brand-toggle send-as work end-to-end against HTT-domain mail flow rule
- 3 remaining new DCE distribution lists created and tested (franchise_owners@ already live)
- Role-based access scopes defined for all 6 role types
- Jamie Baer has Edit access to populate next week

Status 2026-05-15: Phase 1 discovery complete. Phase 2+ pending Tyler decisions on Q1-Q4 raised in session level-set. Awaiting auth grants for Sites.Read.All re-audit + Exchange Online for _fullHTT investigation.

Out of scope (do NOT build):
- Public-facing Delta Crown WordPress site
- HTT Beauty University / WiseTail LMS integration
- DCE-specific Freshdesk instance
- Final branding assets (Jamie's job)
- Design & Construction Manual content
- Member rosters with real owner data

Validation gate: Phase 8 checklist in the prompt must read PASS for all 12 items before this closes.
notes:
2026-05-15 discovery complete (code-puppy-1bc20e). Sites.Read.All re-audit gave us the actual SharePoint inventory we were missing:

HTT tenant: 13 sites. Notable: TheHTTHub (HTT hub for golden-child reference), and msteams_7f6ca9 named 'Delta Crown Operations' which was NOT in our docs.

DCE tenant: 15 sites. Full hub+spoke architecture (dce-hub + 4 brand sites + 4 corp service sites) is live and matches README baseline. Two duplicate DeltaCrownExtensions group sites remain (per docs/duplicate-delta-crown-extensions-groups-review.md).

Blocking decision for Tyler: Friday prompt §3 says hub goes 'in the HTT tenant under the Delta Crown brand domain path.' But dce-hub already exists in the DCE tenant with full spokes. Three possible reconciliations are documented in docs/friday-2026-05-15-audit-findings.md Finding 6 — DCE tenant primary, HTT tenant primary, or hybrid. Cannot start Phase 5 folder scaffolding until this is resolved.

Secondary blocking decision: disposition of httbrands.sharepoint.com/sites/msteams_7f6ca9. Audit captured this exists; owner / purpose unknown from repo state.

Audit evidence: docs/friday-2026-05-15-audit-findings.md committed. Local-only: .local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T2058*-*.json
2026-05-15 cross-link: a parallel owners-connect surface (Crown Connection, separate from this hub's broader-staff scope) was launched per Kristin's SharePoint-launch ask. Tracked separately as DeltaSetup-of8 (closed). The two surfaces are distinct:
- This umbrella (DeltaSetup-2dq) = /sites/dce-hub for ALL staff, operational content
- DeltaSetup-of8 = /sites/CrownConnection for OWNERS ONLY, leadership/peer comms

Follow-up beads from the owners-connect work:
- DeltaSetup-yz2: add OwnerConnection@deltacrown.com alias (needs DCE-native EXO admin)
- DeltaSetup-69v: cross-brand owners-connect cleanup (TLL/Bishops/Frenchies to spec)

Naming spec: docs/naming-conventions/owners-connect-cross-brand.md

Target DDG:

- Email: franchise_owners@deltacrown.com

title: Phase 3.0 pass 2: compress White Glove and problem evidence sections
description: Continue the Phase 3.0 homepage redesign by converting oversized narrative/evidence sections into decision-useful digest components. Compress White Glove on index.html into a compact target day-one experience card that links to operations.html, and convert the problem/folder section into an evidence digest with optional details for audited examples.
acceptance_criteria: Homepage White Glove section is compact and action-oriented; problem section presents evidence with implication/action; large decorative evidence is collapsed or moved; audits pass; changes pushed.
notes: Follow-up from experience-architect-session-d987b0. Phase 3.0 pass 1 replaced ticker with Decision Brief and simplified hero routing.
close_reason: Completed Phase 3.0 pass 2: compressed the homepage White Glove scrollytelling into a compact Target Day-One Experience decision card, added explicit current gaps and ops route, replaced the large problem/folder wall with a Current-State Evidence digest, contextual metrics, implication/action cards, and a details disclosure for audited folders. Audits pass.

title: Complete manual WCAG 2.2 AAA certification pass
description: Perform a manual/browser-assisted WCAG 2.2 AAA review of index.html, operations.html, and msp.html before making any public AAA compliance claim. Cover AAA criteria that cannot be fully proven statically: focus not obscured enhanced, link purpose in context/link-only where applicable, section headings, reading level/plain language, animation from interactions, target spacing, keyboard order, screen-reader behavior, and page behavior with browser zoom/text spacing.
acceptance_criteria: Manual checklist completed with evidence, any exceptions documented as not applicable or remediated, and an explicit final claim decision: AAA compliant, AAA-ready with exceptions, or AA only.
notes:
Blocked until a human/browser QA pass is scheduled. Static audit and Playwright smoke audit pass as of 2026-05-07.
Checklist scaffolded at research/wcag-22-aaa-readiness-2026-05-07/AAA-CERTIFICATION-CHECKLIST.md (380 lines).

Pre-filled with all statically/automatically verifiable evidence:
- Section A (15 items) verified by static + browser-smoke + axe audits (all green).
- Section B (16 manual items) each have how-to-test + expected + evidence slots.
- Section C lists N/A items with rationale (no audio/video/forms/timers).
- Section D pre-flags two honest gaps for the reviewer to decide on:
  * 3.1.4 Abbreviations: 33 abbrs in body copy, 0 <abbr> markup.
  * 3.1.5 Reading Level: FK grade 10-13 vs AAA ~9 target.
- Section E is the sign-off form (AAA / AAA-ready-with-exceptions / AA only).

Companion follow-up bead DeltaSetup-1kp filed for 4.1.2 aria-prohibited-attr cleanup (28 nodes axe surfaced).

Next action is human/browser/AT testing per Section B. Reassigning to Tyler.

title: Verify completion status and WCAG 2.2 AAA readiness
description: Audit the current GitHub Pages site after the design-system and MSP brief work. Determine what is complete, what remains, and whether the public pages can honestly claim WCAG 2.2 AAA compliance. Do not claim AAA unless validated against applicable AAA success criteria, including contrast, focus, motion, readability, headings/landmarks, target size, and responsive behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Report created with completion matrix, AAA compliance claim status, verified passes, verified failures/unknowns, and remediation beads for any remaining work.
notes: Owner/agent: code-puppy-13b0ad. Pages in scope: index.html, operations.html, msp.html. Static site deployed from gh-pages.
close_reason: Completed AAA readiness alignment report and remediation pass. Added static and browser smoke audit scripts; both pass. Fixed AAA contrast token gaps, skip-link focus behavior, and heading-order warnings. Formal AAA claim remains pending manual certification in DeltaSetup-9gq; axe/pa11y automation tracked in DeltaSetup-did.

title: Add 7 unsynced HTT users to Crown Connection after next cross-tenant sync cycle
description:
These 7 HTT licensed users were not in DCE's directory when we bulk-added HTT corp users to Crown Connection (DeltaSetup-of8). They should sync via the next cross-tenant sync cycle (~40 min after each HTT-side change).

Pending (state captured 2026-05-15):
- jamie.baer@httbrands.com (Jamie Baer, created 2026-03-10) - Tyler's collaborator on this work, HIGH priority
- lindsey.anarumo@httbrands.com (Lindsey Anarumo, 2026-04-14)
- kennedy.dickie@httbrands.com (Kennedy Dickie, 2026-03-25)
- mj.ramsbacher@httbrands.com (MaryJo Ramsbacher, 2026-02-18)
- tracey.tomlinson@httbrands.com (Tracey Tomlinson, 2026-03-16)
- jill.holderfield@httbrands.com (Jill Holderfield, 2026-03-10)
- yara.gonzalez@httbrands.com (Yara Gonzalez Ramirez, 2024-08-15) - ANOMALY: has department='HTT Brands Corporate' and old account, should have synced; investigate why she's missing from DCE directory

Suspected root cause for 6 of 7: cross-tenant sync scope filter excludes users with no department set. Most are recently-created HTT joiners awaiting attribute population (department, jobTitle).

Action: just re-run tools/expand-crown-connection-htt-corp.py after the next sync cycle. The script is idempotent — picks up newly-synced users without re-adding existing members.

For Yara: needs separate investigation into the cross-tenant sync configuration in HTT to understand why she's excluded.
notes:
2026-05-15 23:53 UTC RESOLVED: All 7 HTT users invited into DCE via Graph /invitations (silent invite, sendInvitationMessage=False, matching the 2026-03-04 bulk pattern). All flipped userType=Guest -> Member. All 7 then added to Crown Connection via the expansion script.

Final Crown Connection state: 57 members (5 DCE owners + 52 HTT corp), 3 owners (Tyler, Kristin, Jenna). 100% audience coverage.

KEY ROOT-CAUSE FINDING: HTT and DCE are NOT in a Multi-Tenant Organization, and there is NO cross-tenant synchronization job between them (verified via Graph crossTenantAccessPolicy + servicePrincipals search). The 74 HTT-origin users in DCE came from a one-time manual B2B invite event on 2026-03-04 (verified via createdDateTime + creationType=Invitation on sample users). The 7 'missing' users were simply not included in that bulk (6 hadn't been hired yet, 1 was missed).

Implication for ongoing operations: new HTT corporate hires will NOT automatically appear in DCE. Each must be invited as part of HR/onboarding (or via periodic backfill). Filed tooling: tools/invite-htt-users-to-dce.py for repeatable single-user or bulk invites.

This bd is being closed; the broader 'establish ongoing HTT->DCE onboarding process' need lives at DeltaSetup-jch (repurposed).

title: Root-cause _fullHTT Scot Cannon ghost membership drift
description:
_fullHTT@httbrands.com (display: _All-Corporate Team, MailUniversalDistributionGroup _FullHTT20240913183055) still resolves Scot.Cannon@httbrands.com as a member after his departure. Friday 2026-05-15 read-only audit confirmed 39-member roster includes Scot as a UserMailbox match. The 2026-05-12 B1 safe guest cleanup did not address this (Scot is an internal mailbox, not a guest).

Per Friday SharePoint hub delivery prompt section 4.6, this is a HARD BLOCKER for propagating the dynamic distribution group pattern to Delta Crown lists. Until root-caused, do not extend dynamic-group automation into DCE; use manual/static groups as workaround.

Evidence:
- .local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T171157Z-HTT-fullHTT-and-rules-summary.json
- .local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T171157Z-HTT-fullHTT-static-members.csv

Likely causes to investigate (read-only first):
1. Scot's HTT user is still enabled / licensed / has active mailbox
2. Soft-deleted but matching the recipient filter
3. Mailbox indexing lag from departure
4. Shared mailbox or alias creating phantom membership
5. _FullHTT is a static MailUniversalDistributionGroup (not dynamic) and Scot is a literal hard-coded member — meaning B1 guest cleanup wouldn't have touched it because it isn't filter-driven

Out of scope: actual HTT offboarding remediation belongs in HTT-side tooling, not DeltaSetup. This bead closes when root cause is identified, documented, and the appropriate remediation issue is filed in the correct repo.
notes:
2026-05-15 initial triage (code-puppy-1bc20e): re-reading audit evidence shows _All-Corporate Team is a static MailUniversalDistributionGroup, NOT a DynamicDistributionGroup. Confirmed from .local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T171032Z-exchange-summary.json (RecipientTypeDetails=MailUniversalDistributionGroup) and the audit script writing -static-members.csv. Scot.Cannon@httbrands.com is therefore a hard-coded member that was simply not removed during HTT offboarding — there is no recipient-filter / attribute-sync drift to debug.

Impact on Friday SharePoint hub plan: the prompt §4.6 'do not propagate dynamic-group pattern to DCE until drift is resolved' premise is invalidated. The DCE DDGs (allstaff@, managers@, stylists@, franchise_owners@) are real DynamicDistributionGroup objects keyed on Company/Department/Title; they are unaffected by _fullHTT's static-membership problem. Friday SharePoint hub work is NOT blocked by this bead.

Remaining action for this bead: file HTT-side offboarding remediation issue (correct repo TBD with Tyler — likely HTT operations tooling or Cross-Tenant-Utility offboarding workflow). Close this bead once handoff is filed.
2026-05-15 root-cause complete (code-puppy-1bc20e). Live Exchange Online probe with delegated org httbrands.onmicrosoft.com confirmed:

(1) _fullHTT@httbrands.com is MailUniversalDistributionGroup (static), GroupType Universal, MemberJoinRestriction=Closed, MemberDepartRestriction=Closed, ManagedBy two GUIDs. WhenChanged 2026-05-05. Not dynamic; not filter-driven. The 'drift' framing in the Friday prompt §4.6 was based on a faulty premise.

(2) Scot.Cannon@httbrands.com is fully active: AccountDisabled=false, WhenSoftDeleted=null, RemotePowerShellEnabled=true, RecipientType=UserMailbox, Department='HTT Brands Corporate', Title='Vice President of Field Support', last WhenChanged 2026-04-04. There has been no offboarding — neither account-disable, nor mailbox soft-delete, nor distribution-group removal, nor GAL-hide.

Conclusion: this is not a DeltaSetup engineering problem. Either Scot hasn't actually departed (in which case the premise was wrong) or HTT offboarding never ran for him (HR/IT gap on the HTT side). DCE DDGs are independent of _fullHTT and unaffected.

Evidence committed: docs/friday-2026-05-15-audit-findings.md (findings 3 + 4). Local-only evidence: .local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T205855Z-HTT-{fullHTT-group-detail,fullHTT-members-detail,scot-cannon-state}.*

Recommend closing as handed-off pending Tyler call on which workflow owns the HTT offboarding remediation.
2026-05-15 OFFBOARDING COMPLETE (code-puppy-1bc20e). Tyler authorized full end-to-end offboarding. All actions applied and verified:

Exchange (via Connect-ExchangeOnline -Device, tyler.granlund-admin@httbrands.com):
- Set-Mailbox -Type Shared: APPLIED (was UserMailbox)
- Kayla.Bramlet@httbrands.com FullAccess already present (from prior drive-transfer setup) - SKIPPED
- HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled $true: APPLIED
- Remove-DistributionGroupMember _fullHTT@httbrands.com: APPLIED

Graph (via az CLI session token — Microsoft.Graph public-client device-code OAuth flow returned Authorization_RequestDenied on accountEnabled PATCH; az-cli fallback succeeded):
- accountEnabled false: APPLIED (HTTP 200)
- revokeSignInSessions: APPLIED (HTTP 200, value=true) [required explicit Content-Length: 0 header to avoid HTTP 411]
- assignLicense remove 3 SKUs: APPLIED (HTTP 200, assignedLicenses now [])

Final verified state (.local/reports/friday-sharepoint-hub-audit/20260515T212711Z-scot-offboard-FINAL.json):
{
  "displayName": "Scot Cannon",
  "accountEnabled": false,
  "assignedLicenses": []
}

Note: Kayla was found at Kayla.Bramlet@httbrands.com (single-t spelling), not Kayla.Bramlett. Side discovery: KaylaBramletScotCannon@httbrands.com SchedulingMailbox exists (shared calendar resource) — out of scope for this bead.

Also documented in script header (tools/offboard-scot-cannon.ps1): the public-client device-code flow gotcha for PATCH /users accountEnabled, and az CLI workaround pattern for future offboardings.

Closing this bead — actual root cause was 'no offboarding ever ran for Scot,' not '_fullHTT dynamic-group drift.' Both pieces now resolved.

Validation:

This should replace the prior corrupt/bad workbook.

title: Add plain-language summary disclosure to public pages (WCAG 3.1.5)
description:
Section D2/B13 of the AAA cert checklist flagged WCAG 3.1.5 (Reading Level, AAA) as a likely fail: full-page Flesch-Kincaid grade 10.4-12.9 vs AAA target ~9. WCAG 3.1.5 accepts a 'supplementary version' that does not require advanced reading.

Implementation: added a discreet <details> disclosure as the first child of <main> on each public page (index.html, operations.html, msp.html). The disclosure summary reads 'Read this page in plain language' and contains 6-7 short sentences covering the page topic at FK grade 3-4. Light surface, gold accent toggle, keyboard accessible by default (native <details>), respects prefers-reduced-motion.

Verification:
- Plain-summary block FK grades: 3.2 (index), 3.0 (ops), 4.0 (msp) — all below grade 9 target.
- Full page FK unchanged (10-12) but that is acceptable per 3.1.5 since the supplementary version is now available.
- All three quality gates still green.

Acceptance: each public page has a discoverable, keyboard-accessible plain-language summary; FK grade of summary block is <= 9; checklist Section D2/B13 marked resolved.

title: Add <abbr> markup for technical abbreviations on public pages (WCAG 3.1.4)
description:
Section D2 of the AAA cert checklist flags 33 distinct uppercase abbreviation candidates in body copy across index.html/operations.html/msp.html with zero <abbr> markup. WCAG 2.2 SC 3.1.4 Abbreviations (AAA) requires a mechanism for identifying the expanded form.

Scope: wrap the FIRST text occurrence per page of each technical abbreviation in <abbr title='Expansion'>ABBR</abbr>. Brand and product names (DCE, HTT, HTTHQ, TLL, BCC, FMN, MOSA, P2, TEMP, ID) are out of scope -- they are proper nouns defined contextually.

Real abbreviations to wrap: ADR, CSP, DKIM, DLP, DMARC, E2E, EDR, HR, IA, IT, M365, MFA, MSP, PDF, RACI, RMM, SKU, SPF, SSO, STRIDE.

Acceptance: page validates, all three quality gates green, abbreviation candidates list in checklist updated, Section D2 marked resolved.

title: Audit and remediate aria-label on non-semantic divs (axe aria-prohibited-attr)
description:
axe-core flags 28 nodes across index.html (19), operations.html (8), msp.html (1) where aria-label is applied to plain <div> elements with no explicit role. Per ARIA in HTML, aria-label is prohibited on generic elements with no role because there is no semantic for the name to apply to. axe marks these incomplete (not violations) because it cannot fully prove implicit name sources, but the underlying rule is real for WCAG 2.2 AAA.

Typical offenders are landmark-ish containers like .hero-systems, .project-route-actions, .decision-grid, .op-command, .op-impact-grid, .msp-status-grid.

For each: choose either (a) add a meaningful role such as role=group, role=region, or role=list with appropriate descendants; (b) replace the <div> with a semantic element (<section aria-labelledby> when a visible heading exists); or (c) drop aria-label if the surrounding context already names the region. Re-run python3 tests/accessibility_axe_audit.py and verify the aria-prohibited-attr incomplete count drops to 0 or is explicitly justified.

Acceptance: axe audit reports 0 aria-prohibited-attr nodes OR remaining nodes have a documented exception in the manual AAA checklist.

title: Phase 3.0 pass 3: replace homepage MSP/RACI explainer with routing card
description: Reduce homepage duplication by replacing the full MSP/CSP explainer/RACI flow with a concise route card pointing to msp.html. Keep homepage focused as a project command brief while preserving MSP responsibility detail on the dedicated MSP page.
acceptance_criteria: Homepage contains concise MSP/CSP routing surface; detailed RACI remains on msp.html; navigation remains clear; audits pass; changes pushed.
close_reason: Completed Phase 3.0 pass 3: replaced the homepage MSP/CSP/RACI explainer with a concise MSP/CSP route card. Homepage now summarizes HTT/Sui Generis/Pax8 seams and routes detailed RACI/acceptance content to msp.html. Audits pass.

title: Add automated axe/pa11y accessibility regression to quality gates
description: Add a browser-based automated accessibility runner (axe-core, pa11y, or equivalent) for the three public pages. The existing static and Playwright smoke scripts are useful but do not cover axe rule checks. This should become part of the regular quality gate before claiming accessibility compliance.
acceptance_criteria: A repeatable command exists, runs against index.html/operations.html/msp.html, fails on violations, and is documented in the repo QA instructions.
notes: Can use Playwright + axe-core or pa11y. Keep dependencies minimal and avoid huge generated artifacts.

title: Cross-brand owners-connect SharePoint cleanup (TLL/Bishops/Frenchies parity to spec)
description:
Per docs/naming-conventions/owners-connect-cross-brand.md, the three existing brand owner-connect sites diverge from the canonical spec adopted for DCE. Needs Kristin/leadership decision on whether to enforce retroactive parity.

Existing state:
- Frenchies: display 'Studio Connection', mailNickname OwnerConnection (rename history), primary StudioConnection@frenchiesnails.com, Private — broad membership (119)
- Bishops: display 'Connect', mailNickname Connect, primary Connect@bishops.co, Public (!), /sites/ConnectNow — broad (134)
- TLL: display 'TLL Owners Group', mailNickname TLLOwnersGroup, /sites/TLLOwnersGroup, Private team — owner-only (11)
- DCE (new): display 'Crown Connection', mailNickname CrownConnection, primary CrownConnection@deltacrown.com, /sites/CrownConnection, Private — owner-only (5)

Spec deltas per brand documented in docs/naming-conventions/owners-connect-cross-brand.md §4.

Open questions to resolve before doing the work:
1. Does Kristin want retroactive enforcement (will involve renames + redirects + comms) OR only forward-going consistency?
2. Is 'owners only' or 'owners + managers + staff' the spec membership scope? Brands diverge today.
3. Should a parallel 'Managers' tier get its own spec?

Out of scope until those decisions are made. Filed so it doesn't get lost.
notes: 2026-05-15 Tyler: 'We can pull off on that one.' Parking this for now — leaving open at low priority. Not actively worked.

title: Add OwnerConnection@deltacrown.com alias to Crown Connection group
description:
Crown Connection M365 group (DeltaSetup-XXX) shipped without the cross-brand parity alias OwnerConnection@deltacrown.com.

Reason for deferral: Tyler's HTT admin account is a Graph/Entra Global Admin guest in DCE but has no Exchange role, so Connect-ExchangeOnline -Organization deltacrown.onmicrosoft.com falls through to HTT tenant. To set proxyAddresses on a UnifiedGroup, EXO PowerShell with a DCE-native admin is needed.

Resolution path (when ready):
  Connect-ExchangeOnline -UserPrincipalName Megan.Myrand@deltacrown.com -Device
  Set-UnifiedGroup -Identity CrownConnection -EmailAddresses @{
      add = @('smtp:OwnerConnection@deltacrown.com',
              'smtp:OwnerConnection@deltacrown.onmicrosoft.com')
  }

Verification:
  (Get-UnifiedGroup CrownConnection).EmailAddresses | Where-Object { $_ -like '*OwnerConnection*' }

This alias is nice-to-have for cross-brand parity (Frenchies has OwnerConnection@frenchiesnails.com as alias). Not blocking the Jenna Boden launch — primary CrownConnection@deltacrown.com works.
notes:
2026-05-15 RESOLVED: Added both aliases via Connect-ExchangeOnline -DelegatedOrganization 'deltacrown.onmicrosoft.com' -Device.

Root cause for prior failure: -Organization flag is silently ignored when the connecting user has no NATIVE account in the target tenant (it requires a native UPN, not a #EXT# guest UPN). For cross-tenant Global Admin scenarios, the correct flag is -DelegatedOrganization.

Final state:
  Primary:  SMTP:CrownConnection@deltacrown.com
  Aliases:
    smtp:OwnerConnection@deltacrown.com         <- NEW
    smtp:OwnerConnection@deltacrown.onmicrosoft.com  <- NEW
    smtp:CrownConnection@deltacrown.onmicrosoft.com

Documented the cross-tenant EXO pattern at tools/connect-exo-cross-tenant.md so future tasks don't rediscover it.
